title: 2026 Naval Mine Warfare Essay Contest
description: Address the strategic, operational, and tactical employment of 21st-century U.S. Navy mine warfare systems and what is needed to enable that employment.
text:
The Challenge:
Address the strategic, operational, and tactical employment of U.S. Navy mine warfare systems and what is needed to enable that employment. Essays may examine technological as well as organizational, doctrinal, and industrial challenges in delivering and sustaining mine warfare capability and capacity for U.S. and allied fleets and expeditionary forces.
Challenge Areas:
Integrating mining and mine countermeasures concepts and technologies into evolving warfare areas and warfighting concepts.
Generating and sustaining mine warfare capability and capacity in the face of shipbuilding, sustainment, and resource constraints.
Considering emerging technologies’ influence on the planning, decision-making, and conduct of mine warfare.
Validating and improving the employment, training, maintenance, and sustainment of current MCM platforms.
Submission Guidelines
Open to all contributors -- active-duty military, reservists, veterans, and civilians.
Essays must be no more than 2,500 words, excluding endnotes and sources. Include word count on title page of the essay.
Essays are judged in the blind. Do not include author name(s) on title page or within the essay.
Submit essay as a Word document at www.usni.org/nmwessay no later than 31 March 2026.
Essay must be original and not previously published (online or in print) or being considered for publication elsewhere.
First Prize: $1,500
Second Prize: $750
Third Prize: $500
Selection Process
A joint panel composed of experts from the Mine Warfare Association and the Proceedings editorial staff shall judge essays and choose winners. All essays will be judged in the blind — i.e., the Proceedings staff members and Mine Warfare Association judges will not know the authors of the essays. Since we receive so many submissions (more than 100 per month!), notification of acceptance on one of our platforms can take 4-6 months. We will notify you via email if your essay is selected for a prize or for publication.
Announcement of the Winners
Winners will be published in Proceedings, in print or online.
